facade_description: 6 Lippincott St presents a converted house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance. HCD note: The property at 12 Kensington Avenue has value as an early-twentieth-century vernacular style twostorey commercial addition to a nineteenth-century residential structure that retains some legibility of its original form including south side wall with gabled roofline.
